body_txt: Political correctness ("PC" for short) is discouraging language and behaviors that could be seen as bigoted, offensive, or exclusionary, and replacing it with more inclusive and appropriate terminology. It's strongly associated with social liberalism and progressivism, and has received plenty of pushback from critics on both the left and right. In fact, the term "political correctness" originally started as a pejorative, used by early 20th century socialists to describe comrades who were too rigidly dogmatic. Wherever critique is, ridicule and parody soon follow, and many creators can't resist adding a Take That! depiction of them. Advocates for political correctness are typically lampooned as Moral Guardians , Soapbox Sadies , and Granola Girls who care about policing everyone's language more than anything else, as well as Extreme Doormats who cave to their every demand for fear of offending them. Terminology most people would consider vaguely insensitive at worst is Serious Business to them, and they will fight it at every turn. The topics that parodic PC protestors promote are usually based on real-world progressive causes, but exaggerated by making them fixate on petty or absurd aspects. They may say the term " Black Sheep " is an anti-Black slur, accuse firefighters of ableism for using fire-retardants, or demand bakeries be gender-inclusive and rename ladyfingers to "digit biscuits." For holidays, they may insist on being religiously inclusive by making up generic secular alternatives or by mashing every religion's overlapping holidays into a single celebration. They may even use Insistent Terminology in contexts where it doesn't even make sense, like African-American-and-Caucasian Morality , The LGBTQ+ '90s , or Chow Yun-plus-size . They're not portrayed as particularly evil—at worst, they're well-meaning but pedantic and obnoxious—but they inevitably end up making complete fools of themselves. They may be Windmill Crusaders decrying things nobody is bothered by, even the groups they claim to speak for. They may also be Innocently Insensitive to marginalized people by being incredibly ignorant or patronizing toward them, such as denying their N-Word Privileges , not caring about their perspectives, and telling them what they should find offensive. Ineffectual PC advocates may be contrasted against a Politically Incorrect Hero who, for all their uncouthness, is far better at helping others and inspiring social change. Sister trope to Political Correctness Is Evil , where political correctness and its advocates are harmful or evil, whereas this is simply Played for Laughs . Commonly spouted by a Soapbox Sadie , Straw Feminist , Animal Wrongs Group , and Malcolm Xerox . Compare The War on Straw , Everything Is Racist , Windmill Political , and Politically Correct History . See also Bowdlerise , Sensitivity Training , Have a Gay Old Time , and Kotobagari (which is specifically about the Japanese language). Because this is the subject of many contentious political debates, In-Universe Examples Only please!
